data.title: Ellomay Capital Reports Results for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year of 2025
data.description: Israeli company Ellomay Capital Ltd. (NYSE American: ELLO; TASE: ELLO), specializing in renewable energy projects in Europe, USA, and Israel, released its unaudited consolidated financial results for the last quarter and year concluding on December 31, 2025.

data.title: Data Breach Alert: Edelson Lechtzin LLP Investigates CareCloud, Inc., Data Breach
data.description: In Somerset, New Jersey, on March 31, 2026, legal firm Edelson Lechtzin LLP announced their investigation into data privacy claims related to the recent CareCloud, Inc. data breach. The breach was uncovered by CareCloud on March 16, 2026, impacting their computer network. The law firm specializes in class action cases on a national scale, showing a strong focus on addressing potential issues arising from this incident, with significant implications for affected individuals.

data.title: Global Beauty Market Grows 10% as AI and E-commerce Reshape Consumer Buying
data.description: In Chicago, NielsenIQ (NYSE:NIQ) revealed its State of Beauty 2026 report, indicating a 10% annual growth in the global beauty market. E-commerce sales surged significantly, showing a six-fold increase compared to in-store sales. The report underscores a quick transition towards digitally-driven commerce influenced by AI in major global markets. Consumers are now seeking convenience, personalization, and smooth digital interactions, prompting beauty brands to adapt swiftly to meet these changing demands.

data.title: Douglas Emmett Announces Dates for Its 2026 First Quarter Earnings Results and Live Conference Call
data.description: In Santa Monica, California, Douglas Emmett, Inc. (NYSE:DEI), a real estate investment trust (REIT), revealed plans to unveil its first-quarter earnings for 2026 after the market closes on Tuesday, May 5, 2026. A live conference call is set for Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. PST / 2:00 p.m. EST. Leading the call will be Jordan Kaplan, Chairman and CEO, joined by Peter Seymour, CFO.

data.title: Caltech warns: Quantum computers may threaten Bitcoin & Ethereum keys sooner than predicted.
data.description: Recent studies indicate that fault-tolerant quantum computers may be developed sooner than previously anticipated. This advancement poses a potential risk to the cryptography of popular digital currencies such as Bitcoin and Ethereum. If these quantum machines materialize sooner than expected, they could compromise the security of transactions and private information associated with these cryptocurrencies. This development underscores the need for ongoing research and advancements in cryptography to stay ahead of potential threats.
